title: Authelia
description: Open-source authentication and authorization server providing SSO and multi-factor auth as a companion to reverse proxies via a login portal.
installation: Authelia is an SSO / 2FA portal that protects other apps via your reverse proxy (forward-auth / OpenID Connect); it is not a destination app on its own. It needs a YAML config file that environment variables cannot fully replace - access control rules, the authentication backend (file or LDAP), the session cookie domain, and the SMTP/notifier must be defined in /config/configuration.yml. A persistent volume is mounted at /config to hold that file plus the bundled file-based users database (users_database.yml); seed both on first boot (exec into the pod or pre-populate the volume) or Authelia will exit at startup. Production state is stored in the postgres add-on (set AUTHELIA_STORAGE_POSTGRES_* - rotate the password) and session data in the valkey add-on (Redis-compatible, no password). ROTATE the three secret env values (AUTHELIA_SESSION_SECRET, AUTHELIA_STORAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY, AUTHELIA_IDENTITY_VALIDATION_RESET_PASSWORD_JWT_SECRET) with 'openssl rand -hex 32' before going live. Email-based flows (password reset, second-factor registration) require an SMTP server configured under notifier in the config file. Authelia listens on HTTP port 9091; TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress.
